req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct concern areas: (1) happy-path partial-commit semantics where only valid rows are imported, (2) strict error responses for unsupported media type and malformed headers, and (3) boundary behaviour at row-count edges. I selected EP for the happy-path mixed CSV (valid + invalid rows in one file) because it exercises the core equivalence class of 'partially-valid input'. NEG covers both 415 and 422 error pathways enumerated in the spec. BVA targets the degenerate boundary of a CSV that contains exactly the header row and zero data rows, probing the lower bound of importable content. A fourth DT case captures the webhook emission side-effect for the positive commit path. A fifth STC case walks the state machine from unauthenticated → authenticated → import-submitted → result-visible. Together they satisfy the mandatory minimum of positive, negative, and boundary coverage while honouring ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4 design techniques.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors
preconditions: Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
No employee with email 'valid@example.com' pre-exists in the system
actions: Build a CSV with two valid rows and one row missing a required field
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
expected: Response body contains 'errors' list with exactly 1 entry
Error entry references the row number of the invalid row
trace: R-WEB-099: error report generated per row
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_hr_admin_session, build_csv_bytes, base_url

def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors():
    session = make_hr_admin_session()
    csv_content = build_csv_bytes(
        headers=["first_name", "last_name", "email", "department"],
        rows=[
            ["Alice", "Smith", "valid1@example.com", "Engineering"],
            ["Bob", "Jones", "valid2@example.com", "HR"],
            ["Bad", "Row", "", ""],
        ],
    )
    resp = session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 2
    assert isinstance(body["errors"], list)
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    assert body["errors"][0]["row"] == 3

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST a plain-text file with Content-Type: application/json to /employees/import
expected: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type is returned
Response body contains a human-readable error message
trace: R-WEB-099: error pathway — 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_hr_admin_session, base_url

def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415():
    session = make_hr_admin_session()
    payload = b'{"employees": []}'
    resp = session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=payload,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a CSV that omits the required 'email' column header
POST it to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
expected: Response body identifies the missing required column
trace: R-WEB-099: error pathway — 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_hr_admin_session, build_csv_bytes, base_url

def test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422():
    session = make_hr_admin_session()
    csv_content = build_csv_bytes(
        headers=["first_name", "last_name", "department"],
        rows=[["Alice", "Smith", "Engineering"]],
    )
    resp = session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body)
    assert "email" in error_text.lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_header_only_csv_imports_zero_rows
preconditions: Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a CSV containing only the header row and no data rows
POST it to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
expected: Response body shows imported_count equal to 0
Response body shows errors list is empty
trace: R-WEB-099: valid rows imported (zero is the lower boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_hr_admin_session, build_csv_bytes, base_url

def test_bulk_import_header_only_csv_imports_zero_rows():
    session = make_hr_admin_session()
    csv_content = build_csv_bytes(
        headers=["first_name", "last_name", "email", "department"],
        rows=[],
    )
    resp = session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        body = resp.json()
        assert body.get("imported_count", 0) == 0
        assert body.get("errors", []) == []

tests.name: test_bulk_import_valid_csv_emits_webhook_event
preconditions: Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
Webhook receiver mock is running and registered for 'employee.imported' events
actions: Register a mock webhook endpoint via <fixture> webhook_receiver
POST a valid single-row CSV to /employees/import
Poll the webhook receiver for the emitted event
expected: HTTP 200 is returned from the import endpoint
Webhook receiver captures exactly one 'employee.imported' event
Event payload contains the imported employee email
trace: R-WEB-099: additional variant semantics — webhook event emitted for external consumers
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import make_hr_admin_session, build_csv_bytes, base_url
from <fixture> import webhook_receiver

def test_bulk_import_valid_csv_emits_webhook_event(webhook_receiver):
    session = make_hr_admin_session()
    csv_content = build_csv_bytes(
        headers=["first_name", "last_name", "email", "department"],
        rows=[["Carol", "Danvers", "webhook@example.com", "Operations"]],
    )
    resp = session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    events = webhook_receiver.wait_for_events(event_type="employee.imported", timeout=5)
    assert len(events) >= 1
    payloads = [str(e) for e in events]
    assert any("webhook@example.com" in p for p in payloads)

tests.name: test_bulk_import_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_result
preconditions: No active session exists at test start
Valid hr-admin credentials are available via <fixture> hr_admin_credentials
Target app is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Start with an unauthenticated HTTP session
Authenticate as hr-admin to transition to authenticated state
POST a valid CSV to /employees/import to transition to import-submitted state
Assert response body to confirm transition to result-available state
expected: Response body confirms imported_count > 0 in result-available state
trace: R-WEB-099: precondition role:hr-admin enforced across state transitions
STC: states — unauthenticated → authenticated → import-submitted → result-available
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from <helper_fn> import login_as_hr_admin, build_csv_bytes, base_url

def test_bulk_import_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_result():
    anon = requests.Session()
    csv_content = build_csv_bytes(
        headers=["first_name", "last_name", "email", "department"],
        rows=[["Eve", "Torres", "state@example.com", "Finance"]],
    )
    r1 = anon.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert r1.status_code in (401, 403)
    auth_session = login_as_hr_admin()
    r2 = auth_session.post(
        f"{base_url()}/employees/import",
        data=csv_content,
        headers={"Content-Type": "text/csv"},
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    body = r2.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] >= 1
